prompt:
I'm building Rosetta for the IBM Bob Hackathon. Solo build, ~45 hours remaining, 40 Bobcoin budget (~37 remaining after this planning session), critical to stay efficient.

PROJECT GOAL
A multi-page Streamlit web application that connects to a relational database, inspects its schema, and generates an interactive onboarding experience for a new developer joining the team. The user provides a connection string in the app and navigates a structured onboarding document with live interactive features.

CHALLENGE ALIGNMENT
IBM Bob Hackathon challenge: "Turn idea into impact faster - Get up to speed on existing code quickly, generate documentation, reduce repetitive tasks." Rosetta replaces the first week of manual database reverse-engineering with an interactive generated experience.

CONSTRAINTS
- Python 3.11+
- Solo developer, 45 hours remaining
- 40 Bobcoin budget total, currently ~37 remaining, target ~35 spent on build
- Must produce: working prototype with public URL, video, pitch deck, exported Bob session report
- MIT licensed, public GitHub repo: github.com/Raven-V1/rosetta
- Demo database: AdventureWorks2025 on local SQL Server 2025 (confirmed accessible)
- Deployment target: Streamlit Community Cloud (free tier)

PRODUCT SHAPE
A multi-page Streamlit app, not a CLI tool. Seven pages:
1. Home/Connect - landing page with connection form
2. Overview - auto-generated database summary
3. Schema Map - interactive diagram of tables grouped by function with relationship lines
4. Start Here - 3-5 most important tables ranked with reasoning
5. First Queries - 10-15 annotated queries, each clickable to run live and show results
6. Glossary - every table with one-line description, searchable, click for column details
7. Download - export full onboarding as single Markdown file

USER FLOW
1. User opens app at the public URL
2. User enters SQL Server connection string on Home page
3. App connects, runs full introspection (target: under 60 seconds for AdventureWorks)
4. User navigates pages via sidebar, all data already loaded into session state
5. User clicks queries to run them live, sees results in tables
6. User clicks tables in Glossary to drill into columns
7. User exports Markdown from Download page

DELIVERABLES TO PLAN (produce in project root)
1. plan.md - implementation plan with:
   - System architecture diagram (text-based, mermaid syntax)
   - Component breakdown with single-responsibility modules
   - Data flow from connection to rendered page
   - Tech stack with specific library choices and rationale (pyodbc vs pymssql, networkx vs pyvis vs streamlit-agraph for the diagram, etc.)
   - Three-tier breakdown: Tier 1 = pages 1, 2, 6, 7 + working deployment; Tier 2 = pages 3, 4, 5 with interactivity; Tier 3 = polish and stretch
   - Bobcoin budget per component (target total 35 coins)
2. decisions.md - architectural decisions with rationale, for the session report
3. Recommended file/folder structure

CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS
- Modular design: each of the seven pages must be implementable independently so I can build, test, and deploy in parallel without breaking other pages
- All database queries must use parameterized SQL or schema-safe operations (no SQL injection vectors)
- Streamlit session state must hold the introspection results to avoid re-querying the database on every page navigation
- The Schema Map diagram is the visually distinctive feature - it must work with AdventureWorks (~70 tables) without becoming unreadable. Recommend a specific library and approach.
- Bob's session report must show substantial agentic work; design the build sequence so Bob is generating real components, not boilerplate

Please ask any clarifying questions before producing the plan. Then produce a thorough plan I can execute against. Be specific about library choices because I cannot afford to discover mid-build that pyvis doesn't work in Streamlit Cloud.
